response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong testing discipline and broad polyglot experience, but the resume shows minimal direct work in the core stack required for this role. Java and Kotlin appear only in passing (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tooling years ago), there is no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience, no JVM internals depth, and no IDE/language tooling background. Their strongest languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — a poor match for an enterprise JVM IDE position.
strengths: Deep experience building complex, large-scale systems and developer tooling
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing libraries
Has shipped C/C++ components for a commercial IDE (NI LabWindows/CVI)
concerns: No demonstrated Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience
Java/Kotlin exposure is marginal and dated; JVM internals not evidenced
Core recent work is TypeScript/Node and DLang, not the JVM ecosystem

key_factors.factor: Lack of Spring/JVM enterprise framework experience
Prior IDE component development at National Instruments
